title: 8.0 Business Startups and Innovation
description: Quiz covering Unit 8 of Grade 10 Economics: innovation, business startups, types of business organizations, and business feasibility analysis.

questions.question: What does organizational innovation refer to?
options: Implementing a new or improved production or delivery approach
Developing a new organizational strategy that changes business practices, workplace organization, and external relationships
Introducing a new or improved good or service
Developing a new marketing strategy that changes product design or packaging
questions.correctAnswer: Developing a new organizational strategy that changes business practices, workplace organization, and external relationships

questions.question: What is process innovation about?
options: Developing a new marketing strategy
Creating a new organizational structure
Implementing a new or improved production or delivery approach
Introducing a new good or service to the market
questions.correctAnswer: Implementing a new or improved production or delivery approach

questions.question: What defines product innovation?
options: Changes in operational methods and techniques used
The introduction of a new or improved good or service
Development of a new sustainability strategy
A new approach to workplace organization
questions.correctAnswer: The introduction of a new or improved good or service

questions.question: What is marketing innovation?
options: Developing a new marketing strategy that changes product design, packaging, price, or promotion
Implementing new software for customer support processes
Reducing environmental impacts of production
Improving technical specifications of a product
questions.correctAnswer: Developing a new marketing strategy that changes product design, packaging, price, or promotion

questions.question: What is eco-innovation?
options: Innovation focused on improving user experience
Innovation resulting in significant progress towards the UN 2030 sustainable development goals
Innovation that generates immediate sales revenue
Innovation limited to changes in software technology
questions.correctAnswer: Innovation resulting in significant progress towards the UN 2030 sustainable development goals

questions.question: According to Schumpeter, what is creative destruction?
options: A method for calculating business profits
A strategy for maintaining market equilibrium
The process where innovation leads to market chaos, making old products obsolete and creating new industries
A government policy that supports failing businesses
questions.correctAnswer: The process where innovation leads to market chaos, making old products obsolete and creating new industries

questions.question: What is the general definition of a startup?
options: A business that has been operating for more than ten years
A company in the initial stages of operations that expects significant growth in the near future
Any business with fewer than fifty employees
A business that focuses only on local markets
questions.correctAnswer: A company in the initial stages of operations that expects significant growth in the near future

questions.question: How do startups primarily differ from small businesses in terms of growth?
options: Startups focus on top-end revenue and rapid growth, while small businesses are content operating in a small portion of a large market
Startups avoid seeking market share, while small businesses aim for global dominance
Startups have restrictions on growth, while small businesses have unlimited growth potential
Startups prioritize steady profits, while small businesses prioritize rapid expansion
questions.correctAnswer: Startups focus on top-end revenue and rapid growth, while small businesses are content operating in a small portion of a large market

questions.question: When do startups typically become profitable?
options: Within the first three months of operation
Immediately after receiving seed funding
It sometimes takes years for a startup to make its first dollar of profit
As soon as they hire their first employee
questions.correctAnswer: It sometimes takes years for a startup to make its first dollar of profit

questions.question: What is seed funding in the context of startups?
options: A loan provided by a bank at zero interest
Money obtained by approaching angel investors who receive a share of the business in return
Funds raised by selling company products in advance
Government grants provided to all new businesses
questions.correctAnswer: Money obtained by approaching angel investors who receive a share of the business in return

questions.question: Which company is mentioned in the textbook as a successful startup with a market value of 50 billion dollars?
options: Tesla
Amazon
Uber
Netflix
questions.correctAnswer: Uber

questions.question: What is a sole proprietorship?
options: A business owned by two or more partners
A form of business where a single individual owns, manages, takes profits, and bears losses
A business organization with a separate legal personality from its owners
A business where ownership is represented by shares of stock
questions.correctAnswer: A form of business where a single individual owns, manages, takes profits, and bears losses

questions.question: What does unlimited liability mean for a sole proprietor?
options: The business and the owner have separate legal identities
The owner's liability is limited to the amount invested in the business
If business assets are insufficient to pay liabilities, the owner's personal property can be used
The owner is not responsible for any business debts
questions.correctAnswer: If business assets are insufficient to pay liabilities, the owner's personal property can be used

questions.question: What is the minimum number of persons required to form a partnership?
options: Three
Five
Two
Four
questions.correctAnswer: Two

questions.question: What is a limitation faced by an unregistered partnership firm?
options: The firm cannot pay taxes
The firm cannot sue outsiders, although outsiders can sue the firm
The firm automatically dissolves after one year
The firm cannot hire employees
questions.correctAnswer: The firm cannot sue outsiders, although outsiders can sue the firm

questions.question: What is a corporation?
options: A business owned and managed by a single individual
A partnership with unlimited liability for all partners
A business organization that has a separate legal personality from its owners, with ownership represented by shares of stock
An informal business arrangement with no legal structure
questions.correctAnswer: A business organization that has a separate legal personality from its owners, with ownership represented by shares of stock

questions.question: What type of liability do stockholders in a corporation enjoy?
options: Unlimited liability
Limited liability
No liability at all
Joint liability with the board of directors
questions.correctAnswer: Limited liability

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of a feasibility study?
options: To plan every operational detail of a new business
To discover if a business or project is feasible before further investment of time, money, and study
To replace the need for a business plan entirely
To guarantee that a business will be profitable
questions.correctAnswer: To discover if a business or project is feasible before further investment of time, money, and study

questions.question: Which of the following is one of the five questions a feasibility study should answer?
options: What is the exact stock price of the company?
Is it profitable or not?
How many employees will be hired in year one?
What color should the logo be?
questions.correctAnswer: Is it profitable or not?

questions.question: Which elements are typically found in a business plan but absent from a feasibility study?
options: Notes on competition
A general description of the business
Specific timelines, detailed budgets with forecasts, resumes of staff, and appendices
Financial projections
questions.correctAnswer: Specific timelines, detailed budgets with forecasts, resumes of staff, and appendices

questions.question: What is cost benefit analysis (CBA)?
options: A method to calculate employee salaries
A process that weighs the sum of benefits of an action against the costs of that action
A technique for marketing products to new customers
A strategy for reducing the number of business competitors
questions.correctAnswer: A process that weighs the sum of benefits of an action against the costs of that action

questions.question: What does net present value (NPV) measure?
options: The total revenue of a company in the current year
The benefits of undertaking an investment by calculating future benefits or costs in terms of their present value
The difference between a company's assets and liabilities
The total number of shares issued by a corporation
questions.correctAnswer: The benefits of undertaking an investment by calculating future benefits or costs in terms of their present value
